2603.20396.title: Compression is all you need: Modeling Mathematics
2603.20396.paper_summary: Human mathematics (HM), the mathematics humans discover and value, is a vanishingly small subset of formal mathematics (FM), the totality of all valid deductions. We argue that HM is distinguished by its compressibility through hierarchically nested definitions, lemmas, and theorems. We model this with monoids. A mathematical deduction is a string of primitive symbols; a definition or theorem is a named substring or macro whose use compresses the string. In the free abelian monoid $A_n$, a logarithmically sparse macro set achieves exponential expansion of expressivity. In the free non-abelian monoid $F_n$, even a polynomially-dense macro set only yields linear expansion; superlinear expansion requires near-maximal density. We test these models against MathLib, a large Lean~4 library of mathematics that we take as a proxy for HM. Each element has a depth (layers of definitional nesting), a wrapped length (tokens in its definition), and an unwrapped length (primitive symbols after fully expanding all references). We find unwrapped length grows exponentially with both depth and wrapped length; wrapped length is approximately constant across all depths. These results are consistent with $A_n$ and inconsistent with $F_n$, supporting the thesis that HM occupies a polynomially-growing subset of the exponentially growing space FM. We discuss how compression, measured on the MathLib dependency graph, and a PageRank-style analysis of that graph can quantify mathematical interest and help direct automated reasoning toward the compressible regions where human mathematics lives.
2603.20396.paper_summary_zh: 人類數學（HM），即人類發現並重視的數學，是形式數學（FM）的一個微小子集，後者是所有有效推導的總和。我們主張，HM 以其通過分層嵌套定義、引理和定理的可壓縮性而區分開來。我們用單元結構來建模這一點。數學推導是一串原始符號；定義或定理是命名的子串或宏，其使用壓縮了這串符號。在自由阿貝爾單元 $A_n$ 中，一個對數稀疏的宏集合實現了表達力的指數擴展。在自由非阿貝爾單元 $F_n$ 中，即使是多項式密集的宏集合也僅產生線性擴展；超線性擴展需要接近最大密度。我們將這些模型與 MathLib 進行測試，MathLib 是一個大型 Lean~4 數學庫，我們將其視為 HM 的代理。每個元素都有一個深度（定義嵌套的層數）、包裝長度（其定義中的標記）和未包裝長度（在完全展開所有引用後的原始符號）。我們發現未包裝長度隨著深度和包裝長度指數增長；包裝長度在所有深度中大致保持不變。這些結果與 $A_n$ 一致，而與 $F_n$ 不一致，支持 HM 佔據指數增長空間 FM 的多項式增長子集的論點。我們討論如何在 MathLib 依賴圖上測量壓縮，以及對該圖的 PageRank 風格分析如何量化數學興趣，並幫助自動推理朝向人類數學所處的可壓縮區域。
2603.20396.author: Vitaly Aksenov et.al.
2603.20396.authors: Vitaly Aksenov, Eve Bodnia, Michael H. Freedman, Michael Mulligan
